form_data.call_to_action: {captured_in_dashboard — review screen showed `PP:` prefix concatenated, artifact-or-merge TBD}

notes: Experiment 3b campaign-registration leg, submission-time capture only. Approval-time data (TCR Campaign ID assignment, status transitions, monthly fee debit timing, number-to-campaign association mechanics, Operator Status population for T-Mo tier + AT&T msg class) to be appended on status change — no approval webhooks observed at submission time. Brand `BTTC6XS` reused from 3a (no second brand registration). Use case LOW_VOLUME chosen with four sub use cases — Sinch's ~22-use-case taxonomy does not map 1:1 to RelayKit's 8 verticals; Phase 5 mapping design is BACKLOG-tracked. Five UX findings recorded in experiments-log.md §Submission UX findings: (1) Supporting Documentation Upload step accepts skip without skip affordance; (2) Privacy/Terms link AND body fields populated together (belt-and-suspenders pattern); (3) `PP:` string appeared concatenated in CTA on review screen — display artifact vs server-side field merge unclear, flagged for post-approval verification; (4) Brand `BTTC6XS` displayed as both Brand Name and Brand ID in campaign detail (cosmetic only); (5) Sinch dashboard exposes ~22 use cases vs RelayKit's 8 verticals — mapping question surfaced. Monthly fee disclosure inconsistency adds a fourth Sinch API/dashboard inconsistency for Session B kickoff verification with BDR (joining brand state machine 5-vs-7, Simplified pricing $10-vs-$6, webhook policy poll-vs-fire from 3a). Submitter contact email redacted ([redacted] in account_id / project_id). Number `+12013619609` was selected in the campaign form but campaign detail post-submission shows 'No Numbers' — number-to-campaign association apparently happens post-approval as a separate step, not at form submission. Form-text fields (description, samples, T&C/Privacy link+body, opt-in doc URL, help/start/stop messages, CTA, additional comments, embedded toggles) marked `{captured_in_dashboard}` rather than transcribed verbatim — schema records the field shape; values live in the Sinch dashboard until approval-time review.
